steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))
CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: The visualized head and body of the pancreas are normal in appearance.

alarm_detect.summary: The abdominal ultrasound only visualized the head and body of the pancreas — the tail was not reported, leaving the pancreas incompletely assessed in a patient with epigastric pain radiating to the back.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is fully capable of imaging the pancreatic tail that was not visualized on ultrasound, and while the physician's reasoning is primarily focused on SBO/internal hernia and ectopic pregnancy, the ordered study will inherently resolve the incomplete pancreatic assessment as part of its comprehensive abdominal survey.
